items.briefing:
Australian police are training to combat crypto scam operations based in Asia-Pacific, a timely reminder that these scams remain a serious threat locally. Separately, Iran has been found using AI to accelerate cyber and influence operations, which could affect Australian organisations and individuals online. A new report also warns that Australia is falling behind on AI governance even as workplaces rapidly adopt AI tools — a gap that poses real risks for organisations without clear policies in place.

Australians are increasingly using AI for financial advice, but unverified AI responses can be inaccurate or misleading. **Practical tip:** If you or your colleagues use AI tools for financial or work decisions, always verify the advice with a qualified professional or trusted official source before acting on it.

featured.title: Australia lags on AI governance as adoption races ahead
featured.quote: Australian organisations lag dangerously behind in AI governance while rapidly increasing AI adoption and spending.
